title: erythroxylum areolatum var. affine
intro: Erythroxylum areolatum var. affine, commonly known as the "Andean coca", is a variety of the Erythroxylum areolatum plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in the Andean region, for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Erythroxylum areolatum var. affine include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as support for digestive issues and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be made into a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and are often used to create medicinal remedies for various ailments.
